feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Absent type deduction
entry.content:
I have created the absent type but the deduction did not occur when running payroll. I need guidance on this matter. Can someone help me with this?

Thanks in Advance

Kaira

entry.title: Message size limit in sap cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

There is always a debate between cloud integration vs ETL tool and when to use what especially when an org has both but managed by different teams.

One of the important factor is message size. However I have not come across any blog or piece of information that mentions the message size limit in cloud integration for each transaction. Can someone share the same?

Also if there are some points of considerations to make for CI vs ETL

@finny_babu @Sriprasadsbhat 

Best Regards

entry.title: JSON fromat Error
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Expected Json format is like Below ( this format is when i run in the postman showing successful) 

 
{
    "sequenceNumber": ,
    "uhlId": " ",
    "dataType": " ",
    "plantCodeLetter": " ",
    "plantIdentifier": " ",
    "fileCreationDate": " ",
    "fileCreationTime": " ",
    "sourceSystem": " ",
    "programVersionOne": " ",
    "programVersionTwo": "string",
    "programVersionThree": "string",
    "tt971Date": " ",
    "tt971Time": " ",
    "completeDataLoad": " ",
    "payload": [ " "
]
} 
As per the expectation i have Created structure Like below

After run the interface also  Json format convertion also coming in SAP PO  like below
{"sequenceNumber": X,"uhlId":"XXX,"dataType":"XXXX","plantCodeLetter":"string","plantIdentifier":"XXX","fileCreationDate":20230127,"fileCreationTime":20230127,"sourceSystem":"XXXX","programVersionOne":"YYYYY","programVersionTwo":"string","programVersionThree":"string","tt971Date":20230127,"tt971Time":"00000","completeDataLoad":"00000","payload":[ " " ]
}
 looks like both are same, But when I use this json format in the Postman tool it is showing as 400 BAD request

Could you please let me know , what changes i have to do,

entry.title: Error while calling SAP API Proxy connected to SAP Gateway service
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The client/consumer of our SAP API Proxy is facing the below issue while calling it.
FYI, API Proxy is connected to backend OData gateway service via API provider. While doing the $metadata call to the SAP gateway, it responded with below error to SAP API M. But after 4 hours the service was back and we see successful message.
But since this is an intermittent issue, we wanted to understand the root cause of the failure. Also where do we get the error logs in SAP gateway for GET $metadata calls that are failed.

Error:

{
  "fault": {
    "faultstring": "Unexpected EOF at target",
    "detail": {
      "errorcode": "messaging.adaptors.http.flow.UnexpectedEOFAtTarget"
    }
  }

Thanks,

Jovita

entry.title: SAP AEM use case : asynch outbound and inbound interface
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to build asynchronous outbound and inbound interface with our SAP Backend.
Regarding asynchronous outbound interface(SAP Backend as sender) I know we can try to use AEM to push the events to partner system(receiver) REST API. How about asynchronous inbound scenario? Can we still use AEM to pull the files/data from partner system or what should partner system support? Should they publish to AMQP queues and then later use CI to pull the messages and use AEM later to push to SAP? (or) should we avoid AEM for inbound interface alone?

For outbound asynchronous interface we have to clarify with partner system how they want.
I understood that if AEM would like to push the data then they need to develop REST API on their side to consume. If partner system would like to pull the data from AEM queues - what is the setup needed from partner system side? Should they use AMQP protocol? Currently, the connectivity options offered by partner system is MQ, CFT, ESB etc., the question is what kind of technology they need to use on their side to pull the messages from AEM Queues? Kindly let me know the points that needs to be aligned with partner system and what kind of development efforts needed from their end(like building REST API) etc., and what kind of details/information needed to be gathered from them? Thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: Connect to On premise system without Cloud Connector
entry.content:
How we can connect with the on-premise system without using a cloud connector?

The on-premise system is located in DMZ, so the customer doesn't allow us to set up a Cloud Connector, they just provide IP and Port (CPI is added to the whitelist).

I have called directly but error.

The message is om.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occurred: connection timed out after 10000 ms: /45.1******:18***.
The MPL ID for the failed message is: AGaCFKrKQQkrmqf3qUd-TmSUcXG2

entry.title: BTP IS Open Connector - Request Body Prehook Script Help
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am in the process of creating the open connector the connection from BTP IS open connector. as per the API requirement I have to pass the below body in order get successful(POST API).  

{ "EmployeeStatus": "Active", "Firstname": "", "Lastname": ""}

Please guide/direct me on writing prehook script to pass the body Jason

Regards, Suresh

entry.title: How to Call an iFlow from SAP CAP Java?
entry.content:
I want to call an Integration Suite IFlow from the SAP CAP Java App.

and I am using BTP Destination for this.

So Basically, I have defined the endpoints of iFlow and creds in the Destination, binded the Destination with the deployed application.
I followed this blog: Consuming external OData service from CAP Java application

My Java Code:

 

package customer.integration_suite.handlers;

import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;

import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.Destination;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.DestinationAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.HttpClientAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationAccessException;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationNotFoundException;

import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;

import org.apache.http.HttpResponse;
import org.apache.http.client.HttpClient;
import org.apache.http.client.methods.HttpGet;

@RestController
public class IntegrationSuiteConnection  implements EventHandler {
    
    @GetMapping("/integration-suite")
    public String connectToIntegrationSuite() {
        String DESTINATION_NAME = "IntegrationSuite";

        try {
            Destination destination = DestinationAccessor.getDestination(DESTINATION_NAME);
            HttpClient client = HttpClientAccessor.getHttpClient(destination);
            HttpResponse httpResponse = null;

            try{
                httpResponse = client.execute(new HttpGet());
                if (httpResponse.getStatusLine().getStatusCode() == 200) {
                    BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                        new InputStreamReader(httpResponse.getEntity().getContent()));
                    StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
                    String line = null;
                    while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                        result.append(line);
                    }
                    reader.close();
                    return result.toString();
                }
            } catch (IOException e) {
                return "IOException: " + e.getMessage();
            }
        } catch (DestinationNotFoundException | DestinationAccessException e) {
            return e.getMessage();
        }

        return null;
    }
}

 

My iFlow is returning just a simple String.

But on hitting the url : https://81fff548trial-dev-integration-suite.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/integration-suite, i am not getting the desired result.
I am getting this on the browser: Failed to get destination.
Please help me to resolve this.

Also, I wanted to know to know, is this even the right way to call an iFlow from the CAP app?
Please let me know about this too.

Thanks,
Abhinav

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget Calls a SAP CPI REST API - Failed with CORS Error
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently trying to make a call to the SAP CPI I-Flow via an SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget.

The I-Flow is triggered via REST-API and everything works so far via Postman with Basic Authentication via ClientID + ClientSecret.

Unfortunately, the REST-API call fails when I try it from the SAP Analytics Cloud/Javascript.

Here you can see my current I-Flow Settings in the REST-API: 

 

Here you can see my current coding in SAP Analytics Cloud/Javascript:

AJAX Call: 

$.ajax({
  url: apiUrl,
  type: "POST",
  headers: {
    "Authorization": "Basic " + btoa(username) + ":" + btoa(password)
  }
})
  .done(function (data){
  $.each(data, function (key, value){

  })
})
  .fail(function (jqXHR, textStatus){
  alert("Error: " + textStatus);
})


I get the following error when executing:

it has something to do with Cors Policy, but i dont know exactly how to handle this problem. 

I also tested it with the Fetch Call but the same error appears. 

Can you help me here what my error could be?

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Best regards

Chris.

entry.title: How to see message by using <xsl:message terminate="yes" in XSLT mapping
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a problem with an XSLT mapping in the Integration Suite:

I want to check for the existence of some mandatory fields in the mapping and, if they are missing, abort the mapping and throw an exception.

Normally, this works using the instruction `<xsl:message terminate="yes">Exception Text</xsl:message>`.

The mapping is aborted in the Integration Suite, but I can't find my message "Exception Text" anywhere. I can only see the error message "Processing terminated by xsl:message at line 5 in XSLTMapping1.xsl," but not my message.

Does anyone know how to see the message in the log?
Otherwise nobody can't see the reason for the cancellation of the mapping.

Kind regards
Thorsten

entry.title: Salesforce Adapter download option not found
entry.content:
I am following the below article but couldn't found download link can anyone help out. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-cloud-integration-with-salesforce-part-1/ba-p/13498594

to download the Salesforce Adapter from this link  https://api.sap.com/package/SalesforceAdapter/overview .

I wanted to build a scenario for integration between SAP ERP and Salesforce using my trial account. 

Thanks,

Hira

entry.title: Handle multiple API Keys in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We are integrating with an external application, which has provided us company specific API key to post data using their API. For Ex : If we are posting data about Company A there is a specific API Key A. If Company B then API Key B. 

I can think of doing this using Value mapping but I would like to know if there is a better approach which is secure as well.  Please suggest

Best Regards

entry.title: There is requirement from user side user want some customized reports on their Gmail id by automatio
entry.content: There is requirement from user side user want some customized reports on their Gmail id by weekly basis. We want to create an automated system. What will be requirement to achieve this task.

entry.title: Integration suite trial account -tile disabled
entry.content:
Hi 

I was trying to create Integration trial account ,have assigned the integration provisional role ,I was able to add capabilities but could find any discover, design, monitor in the integration suite. It appears as attached.

I have tried deleting the subscription, relogging still facing same issue (both normal browser and in incognito mode).Please suggest ideas on this.

Thanks

Priya

entry.title: Connect PO7.5 to Integration Suite?
entry.content:
Hi,

At my customer side we currently have multiple interfaces like ECC backend <=> SAP PO <=> 3rd party
Customer will switch part of the operation to S4HANA and also wants to move from SAP PO to Integration Suite on BTP.

But some of 3rd party connections are really quite complicated and might even be changed in 1 to 2 years, so i feel like the effort to move all of that to IS now, and then still change it later on is not worth the effort.

So what i'm thinking about is a split in some interfaces where we start using IS and PO together.
That will look like S4 <=> IS <=> PO <=> 3rd party.

This way i,
1) only connect S4 to IS (and not also to PO)
2) dont have to worry about the complicated stuff that is between PO and 3rd party
3) take up the work of connecting IS to 3rd party when the time has come for that change they will make

Of course it requires me copy all ICO's on PO to not have ECC as sender (mostly idocs by the way) but have Integration Suite as sender.
So that is some extra work.

Any thoughts on this way of working?
Can i just create HTTP connections between PO and IS to accomplish this scenario?

Any comments are appreciated

Kr

Robert

entry.title: activate IDOC service path in S4 for IDOC receiver connection with CI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on SOAP -> IDOC scenario in Cloud Integration(SAP Integration Suite).

As a pre-requisite, we are trying to establish the IDOC connection from "Cloud Integration" to S4.

But we notice that activation of IDOC service path ‘/sap/bc/srt/idoc’ in SAP backend is needed.

But, our BASIS team declines our request stating that it is not allowed as per our organization hardening guidelines. They are asking to find an alternative way to achieve the required functionality.

Could you please let me know the alternate options available to achieve the requirement without additional efforts like interface redesign etc., Thank you!

Regards,

Ramu

entry.title: how can I streaming in xml to json converter on field level
entry.content:
I am using xml to json converter with following config....

Input xml:

<root>
<records>
<profitCenter>100100</profitCenter>
<validTo>9999-12-31</validTo>
<controllingArea>ABC1</controllingArea>
<validFrom>2023-01-01</validFrom>
<createdOn>2023-11-10</createdOn>
<createdBy>I2193</createdBy>
<longText>Chemicals</longText>
<profitCenterText>Chemicals</profitCenterText>
<profitCenterName>CHEMICALS</profitCenterName>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1000</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1825</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
</records>
<records>
<profitCenter>567327</profitCenter>
<validTo>2024-01-02</validTo>
<controllingArea>ABC1</controllingArea>
<validFrom>2023-11-20</validFrom>
<createdOn>2024-06-26</createdOn>
<createdBy>SAP_WFRT</createdBy>
<profitCenterText>testing</profitCenterText>
<profitCenterName>TESTING</profitCenterName>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1000</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
</records>
</root>

Expected output:

{
"records": [
{
"profitCenter": "100100",
"validTo": "9999-12-31",
"controllingArea": "CSX1",
"validFrom": "2023-01-01",
"createdOn": "2023-11-10",
"createdBy": "I2193",
"longText": "Chemicals",
"profitCenterText": "Chemicals",
"profitCenterName": "CHEMICALS",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes": [
"1000",
"1825"
]
},
{
"profitCenter": "567327",
"validTo": "2024-01-02",
"controllingArea": "CSX1",
"validFrom": "2023-11-20",
"createdOn": "2024-06-26",
"createdBy": "SAP_WFRT",
"profitCenterText": "testing",
"profitCenterName": "TESTING",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes": [
"1000"
]
}
]
}

But getting output as:

{
"records": {
"profitCenter":"100100",
"validTo":"9999-12-31",
"controllingArea":"ABC1",
"validFrom":"2023-01-01",
"createdOn":"2023-11-10",
"createdBy":"I2193",
"longText":"Chemicals",
"profitCenterText":"Chemicals,
"profitCenterName":"CHEMICALS,
"profitCenterCompanyCodes":[
"1000","1825"
]
},
"records":{
"profitCenter":"567327",
"validTo":"2024-01-02",
"controllingArea":"ABC1",
"validFrom":"2023-11-20",
"createdOn":"2024-06-26",
"createdBy":"SAP_WFRT",
"profitCenterText":"testing",
"profitCenterName":"TESTING",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes":[
"1000"
]
}
}

The red record should not come.

entry.title: CPI RFC SCC : An internal server error
entry.content:
Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><rfc:ZSDFU011.Exception xmlns:rfc="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions"><Name>read: Connection broken for conversation ID [00000000] when trying to read SCC header</Name><Text>read: Connection broken for conversation ID [00000000] when trying to read SCC header</Text><Message> <ID/>

entry.title: Availability of Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite trial account
entry.content:
Hi,

As we know Event Mesh is now part of SAP Integration Suite standard license. Do we have any plan to release it in SAP Integration Suite trail account?

Regards,

Souvik

entry.title: Integration Suite (CPI) Synchronous Proxy with Attachment Error
entry.content:
Hello experts

I have the following integration scenario:

1. A synchronous proxy (service consumer) sends purchase order information, including a PDF as attachment to generic proxy dispatcher flow in the IS/CPI. Based on the header values of "SapInterfaceNamespace" and "SapInterfaceName" the processing flow is then called dynamically.

2. The processing flow extracts the attachment and puts it into a property which is thereafter cleared from the message (SAP note 3240138).

3. External REST APIs are called to process the message and also attach the PDF.

4. Based on the processing outcome, a success or failure is returned to the proxy call.

In generally, this works well, but sometimes the proxy call goes on error and the following "PART_BOUNDARY_NOT_FOUND" shows in SXMB_MONI:

In such a case, I see the following log in the proxy dispatcher flow:

Processing exchange ID-10a74212-bf95-45f5-46ff-fb9c-1719846917195-23-2626:
StartTime = Wed Jul 03 08:01:43.915 UTC 2024
StopTime = Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024
ChildCount = 5
ModelStepId = MessageFlow_5
StepId = MessageFlow_5
Activities [
{Activity=sap-processdirect:foo?address=/${header.SapInterfaceNamespace}/${header.SapInterfaceName}, StartTime=Wed Jul 03 08:01:43.915 UTC 2024, StopTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024}
]

Processing exchange ID-10a74212-bf95-45f5-46ff-fb9c-1719846917195-23-2626:
StartTime = Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024
StopTime = Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024
Status = COMPLETED
ChildCount = 6
ModelStepId = EndEvent_2
StepId = EndEvent_2
Activities [
{Activity=setProperty[SAP_MarkMessageAsFailed], StartTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024, StopTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.623 UTC 2024}
{Activity=Exchange ID-10a74212-bf95-45f5-46ff-fb9c-1719846917195-23-2626 completed, StartTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.629 UTC 2024}
]

Sent response from endpoint:
StartTime = Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.625 UTC 2024
Status = FAILED
ChildCount = 7
Cxf.EndpointAddress = /XiEntrySync
Error = Outbound processing in endpoint at /XiEntrySync failed with message "ClassCastException:java.lang.String (loaded by <bootstrap>) cannot be cast to java.lang.Integer (loaded by <bootstrap>)"
ModelStepId = MessageFlow_4
Activities [
{Activity=processEvent[SentResponse], StartTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.625 UTC 2024}
{Activity=log Fault, StartTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.625 UTC 2024, StopTime=Wed Jul 03 08:02:04.625 UTC 2024}
]

Nevertheless, I don't understand why this is happening once in a while but not all the time. When I look at the response payload from the processing flow back to the proxy dispatcher flow then they look exactly the same for the successful flow and the one in error.

Also, a boundary issue would also indicate some kind of issue with the attachment, but again it fails only once in a while when sending with the attachment and never when there's no attachment present.

Anybody having an idea what could be the issue or encountered the same and found a solution?

Thanks in advance,

Daniel
